summary: Malaysia's transition is led by CyberSecurity Malaysia, working with the National Cyber Security Agency (NACSA), which has published a Post-Quantum Cryptography Migration Framework and adoption guidance for government agencies and critical infrastructure, alongside the national MyKriptografi and MySEAL cryptography programmes.
govActors:
CyberSecurity Malaysia | national cyber security specialist agency, publishes the PQC Migration Framework and adoption guidance
NACSA | National Cyber Security Agency, sets national cyber security policy
provenance: CyberSecurity Malaysia PQC Migration Framework + initiatives + adoption recommendations (cybersecurity.my / mykripto.cybersecurity.my, included)

NIST (medium confidence): the migration framework and adoption guidance follow international NIST PQC standards; MyKriptografi/MySEAL are national programmes, not a distinct sovereign PQC algorithm - REVIEW
NACSA roadmap (high-risk by 2030, full by 2035) seen in conference/secondary sources; not set as a timeline here pending the framework PDF text
Model 2026-06-14 (NIST-bloc / taker, High): Migration framework follows NIST; MyKriptografi/MySEAL are national programmes, not a sovereign algorithm.
classificationBasis:

NIST (medium confidence): the migration framework and adoption guidance follow international NIST PQC standards; MyKriptografi/MySEAL are national programmes, not a distinct sovereign PQC algorithm - REVIEW

mainRegulation: CyberSecurity Malaysia / NACSA PQC Migration Framework | national | guidance
obligation: No binding mandate; the national PQC Migration Framework and NACSA guidance direct migration to the NIST baseline. MyKriptografi/MySEAL are national programmes, not a sovereign algorithm.
analyticalNote: Malaysia adopts the NIST baseline via the national framework; no sovereign algorithm.
